items.title: Ilaria Puri Purini - Can Art Help Us Grasp Reality?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Closer To Truth is now on BlueSky! Follow us for insights, updates, and more:
https://shorturl.at/twLQn

Great art is transcendent. Its impact reaches far beyond the art itself and the person who created it. Art can help us perceive reality in new ways and give new meaning to the physical world around us. But what makes art—from music and dance to sculpture and architecture—truly transcendent?

Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

An art historian, curator, and scholar, Ilaria Puri Purini comes to the Academy from the Contemporary Art Society in London, where she has served as curator of programs, working with contemporary artists across Britain, with a special focus on expanding art-historical narratives and practices.

items.title: Jeff Schloss - Is the Future Open?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Show your support for the show with a Closer To Truth merch purchase: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

Why is an ‘open’ future controversial among theologians? If the future is not ‘settled’, if there is no certainty from God’s viewpoint of how future events will turn out, then God’s providence, could not be as sure. If the future is truly open, then not even God could know the future because there are no future facts to know.

Closer To Truth is now on BlueSky! Follow us for updates, perspectives, and more: https://shorturl.at/2eJCn

Jeff Schloss is the Distinguished Professor of Biology, the T.B. Walker Chair of Natural and Behavioral Sciences, and the Director of the Center for Faith, Dr. Schloss has been at Westmont since 1981.

items.author: Closer To Truth

items.title: Denis Alexander - Arguments for Agnosticism?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Contrinute any amount to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

Agnostics do not know whether or not God exists. Theists surmise they’re reprobates. Atheists suppose they’re cowards. Are there different kinds of agnostics? Can agnosticism deepen appreciation for God—what it would take to believe in God and what it would mean if indeed there were a God? A robust, energetic agnosticism is a credit to God if there is one.

Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for daily updates and exciting announcements: https://www.instagram.com/closertotruth/

Dr. Denis Alexander has spent 40 years in the biomedical research community. He is an Emeritus Fellow of St. Edmund’s College, Cambridge and an Emeritus Director of the Faraday Institute for Science and Religion, Cambridge which he co-founded with Bob White in 2006.

items.title: Christopher Knight - How Should Religion Talk to Science?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Sign up for a free Closer To Truth membership to receive special exclusives like early access to new episodes and more: https://closertotruth.com/.

To do good theology, religion must speak with science if only to avoid the embarrassment of supernatural explanations when natural explanations are doing just fine. If religion claims to be an approach to the entirety of existence, then it must embed the discoveries and insights of science. Religion has no choice. At its own risk, religion ignores science.

Find Closer To Truth on Instagram for new videos, announcements, and more: https://www.instagram.com/closertotruth/

Christopher Knight, the Executive Secretary of the International Society for Science and Religion, is based at St.Edmund’s College, Cambridge, England, and has been teaching and writing about the dialogue between science and religion for many years. His most recent book is "The God of Nature: Incarnation and Contemporary Science".

Give what you can to support the show and help Closer To Truth continue exploring life's deepest questions: shorturl.at/OnyRq

items.title: N.T. Wright - How Does Atonement Work?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Shop our special edition merch in honor of the late Steven Weinberg, including mugs and T-shirts: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

Christians are accustomed to hearing that “Jesus died for our sins”. But what would that mean literally? How would atonement work? What would be happening? Is there a process, a sequence of logical steps? One need not be a Christian to appreciate the philosophical obstacles.

Follow Closer To Truth on Facebook and discover daily videos, updates, announcements, and more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l

Nicholas Thomas Wright FRSE, known as N. T. Wright or Tom Wright, is an English New Testament scholar, Pauline theologian and Anglican bishop. He was the bishop of Durham from 2003 to 2010.

items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help support Closer To Truth continue making content like this: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

We offer distinctive approaches to consciousness including cognitive science, phenomenology, and even the implications of aesthetics like fiction and films. We observe the richness of consciousness studies.

Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

Elisabeth Schellekens is a Swedish philosopher and Chair Professor of Aesthetics at Uppsala University. Previously, she was Senior Lecturer at Durham University. Schellekens is known for her works in aesthetics.

items.title: Why is there something rather than nothing?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content: <article>Here’s the ultimate question: what if it were true that everything always and forever had been ‘nothing’? Imagine that not a single thing ever existed–not emptiness, not blankness, not even the existence of emptiness, or the meaning of blankness. If you can image that, now ask yourself: why is there anything at all? Philosopher John Leslie shares his philosophy. #shorts</article>

items.title: Samir Okasha - Philosophy of Biology: Classifying Species & Individuals
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Closer To Truth is now on BlueSky! Follow us for updates, new videos, musings, and more: https://bsky.app/profile/closertotruth.bsky.social

What is the classification system in biology, the taxonomy used to categorize organisms in a structured hierarchy? What are species and individuals in biological and evolutionary contexts, and what is the relationship between them?

items.title: Nancey Murphy - Arguing God from Moral Law?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Like us on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l

Humans have a sense of right and wrong. Does this mean that morality is absolute? And if it’s absolute, would God be needed to make it so? These are separate questions and both require particular answers to arrive at God. Even theologians are perplexed by God and morality; some even admit it.

Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Nancey Murphy is a philosopher and theologian who is Professor of Christian Philosophy at Fuller Theological Seminary in Pasadena, CA. She received a B.A. from Creighton University, a Ph.D. from University of California, Berkeley, and a Th.D. from the Graduate Theological Union.

items.title: Robert John Russell - Does a Fine-Tuned Universe Lead to God?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Contribute what you can to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

We human beings sit roughly midway between the sizes of atoms and galaxies, and both must be so perfectly structured for us to exist. It’s called ‘fine-tuning’ and it’s all so breathtakingly precise that it cries out for explanation. To some, fine-tuning leads to God. To others, there are non-supernatural explanations. Both are startling.

Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

Robert John Russell is founder and Director of the Center for Theology and the Natural Sciences. He is also the Ian G. Barbour Professor of Theology and Science in Residence at the Graduate Theological Union. He has written and edited extensively on possible scientific mechanisms of Christian belief.

items.title: Tom McLeish - Cosmology and Creation
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Show your support for the show with a Closer To Truth merch purchase: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

How do physicists and philosophers who believe in God harmonize the discoveries of cosmology with the doctrine of creation? How does cosmology enhance or erode their belief in God? If God exists, must the universe have had a beginning? Conversely, if the universe had a beginning, must God exist?

Closer To Truth is now on BlueSky! Follow us for updates, perspectives, and more: https://shorturl.at/2eJCn

Thomas Charles Buckland was a British theoretical physicist. His work is renowned for increasing understanding of the properties of soft matter, which matter that can be easily changed by stress—including liquids, foams and biological materials. He was a professor in the Durham University Department of Physics and director of the Durham Centre for Soft Matter, a multidisciplinary team that works across physics, chemistry, mathematics and engineering. He also was the first Chair of Natural Philosophy at the University of York.

items.title: Victoria Harrison - Alternative Concepts of God?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Contribute any amount to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

Is God, if there is a God, a personal, conscious, all-powerful Supreme Being? Some offer radically different concepts of ‘God’, exploring novel ideas of what God may be like. They challenge theism—the God of Judaism, Christianity and Islam—with radically new kinds of gods. Is this ‘heresy’? Or enlarging our vision of what the Ultimate can be like?

Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for daily updates and exciting announcements: https://www.instagram.com/closertotruth/

Victoria S. Harrison is a Professor of Philosophy and Associate Dean of the Faculty of Arts and Humanities at the University of Macau in China. She serves on the Board of Directors of the Center for East Asian and Comparative Philosophy (CEACOP), City University, Hong Kong. There she was a core member of the project Eastern and Western Conceptions of Oneness, Virtue, and Human Happiness (funded by the John Templeton Foundation).

items.title: Susan Schneider on AI, Chatbots, and Consciousness | Closer To Truth Chats
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Professor Susan Schneider discusses the rise of AI chatbots like ChatGPT and others, transhumanism, and the future of artificial intelligence.

MindFest 2025: "Sentience, Autonomy, and the Future of Human-AI Interaction" is a two-day event from March 12-13 presented by the Center for the Future Mind. For more information, and to purchase tickets, click here: https://shorturl.at/pAszN

Dr. Susan Schneider is a philosopher and artificial intelligence (AI) expert. She is the founding director of the Center for the Future Mind at Florida Atlantic University where she also holds the William F. Dietrich Distinguished Professorship. Schneider has also held the Baruch S. Blumberg NASA/Library of Congress Chair in Astrobiology, Exploration, and Scientific Innovation at NASA and the Distinguished Scholar Chair at the Library of Congress.
